issues.summary: MAX_REQUEST_BODY_BYTES = 65536 independently duplicated in two api handler classes rather than centralised in HttpApiUtils
issues.suggestion: Add static final int DEFAULT_MAX_REQUEST_BODY_BYTES = 65536 to HttpApiUtils and reference it from both handlers, or make readBodyWithSizeLimit() use this default when no override is needed. This eliminates the risk of the two values drifting and makes the shared boundary clear.
evidence: CorpusQueryHandlers.java line 27: private static final int MAX_REQUEST_BODY_BYTES = 65536
VisualizationHandlers.java line 25: private static final int MAX_REQUEST_BODY_BYTES = 65536
Both pass this constant to HttpApiUtils.readBodyWithSizeLimit() — the utility that owns the body-reading logic already but not the governing limit

issues.summary: Main.java uses the concrete BlackLabQueryExecutor type in one command handler while using the QueryExecutor interface in another, bypassing the architectural seam inconsistently
issues.suggestion: Change the declaration in handleBlackLabQueryCommand to QueryExecutor executor = new BlackLabQueryExecutor(indexPath) to match the server path and consistently use the interface seam throughout Main.
evidence: handleBlackLabQueryCommand: try (BlackLabQueryExecutor executor = new BlackLabQueryExecutor(indexPath)) — uses concrete class, bypassing the QueryExecutor interface contract
handleBlackLabServerCommand: QueryExecutor executor = new BlackLabQueryExecutor(indexPath) — correctly uses the interface, consistent with how api and exploration layers consume the executor
QueryExecutor extends Closeable, so the try-with-resources in handleBlackLabQueryCommand would work correctly via the interface type — there is no technical reason to use the concrete type

issues.summary: `GrammarConfigLoader.createDefaultEnglish()` implicitly depends on working directory and is called directly in tests without path injection
issues.suggestion: Add a `GrammarConfigLoader.loadFromClasspath(String resource)` overload (or use `fromReader` with a classloader resource stream) and replace `createDefaultEnglish()` calls in tests with it, e.g. `GrammarConfigLoader.fromReader(new InputStreamReader(Objects.requireNonNull(getClass().getResourceAsStream("/grammars/relations.json"))))`. This removes the working-directory dependency from tests while leaving the production `createDefaultEnglish()` path unchanged.
evidence: `createDefaultEnglish()` resolves the config path as `Path.of(System.getProperty("grammar.config", "grammars/relations.json"))` — a relative path evaluated against the JVM working directory at call time.

RelationsValidationTest and HybridAlgorithmTest call `createDefaultEnglish()` directly with no setup, so they silently require the test runner's working directory to be the project root containing `grammars/relations.json`.
There is no test-scoped override via `@BeforeEach` / `@BeforeAll` to set `grammar.config` to a known classpath resource, making the coupling invisible until tests run from a different directory.

issues.summary: RelationPatternBuilder has no direct tests; its lemma-substitution logic is bypassed by stub-based handler tests
issues.suggestion: Add a RelationPatternBuilderTest that verifies buildFullPattern with a real RelationConfig: assert the substituted pattern contains the expected lemma at the correct bracket position, test null/blank headword passthrough, and test the three-arg overload (head + collocate substitution). These are pure string-function tests with no external dependencies.
evidence: RelationPatternBuilder.buildFullPattern is called in SketchHandlers.routeSketchRequest and ConcordanceHandlers.handleConcordanceExamples to build the BCQL pattern passed to the executor
Handler tests use StubQueryExecutor which returns fixed results regardless of the pattern string — so a broken substitution (wrong position index, missing escape) would not be caught
No RelationPatternBuilderTest class exists in src/test/

issues.summary: BlackLabConllUIndexer is entirely @Disabled in CI — the full indexing pipeline has zero runnable automated tests
issues.suggestion: Decouple the indexer test from the external YAML config by either: (a) bundling a minimal conllu-sentences.blf.yaml in src/test/resources so the test can self-configure, or (b) extracting the document-building logic from BlackLabConllUIndexer into a testable method that can be verified without a real BlackLab index (e.g., by asserting on the WPL token structure). Even a smoke test that verifies the indexer constructor creates the output directory without the full format config would provide CI signal.
evidence: BlackLabConllUIndexerTest carries @Disabled at class level: 'Requires conllu-sentences.blf.yaml format config in the project root — not distributed with source'
ConlluConverterTest is the only runnable indexer test (134 lines), but it only covers CoNLL-U → WPL conversion, not the BlackLab indexing step
The full pipeline — CoNLL-U text → ConlluConverter → BlackLabConllUIndexer → on-disk index — has no CI validation

issues.summary: All BlackLabQueryExecutor tests that exercise actual query execution are @Disabled; only static string-helper methods run in CI
issues.suggestion: Consider adding an in-process BlackLab integration test using BlackLab's Searcher.openForWriting() API with a temporary directory and a programmatically-defined document format (no YAML file needed). Index two or three short sentences in the @BeforeAll, then assert that executeCollocations and executeBcqlQuery return the expected lemmas. This keeps the test self-contained and CI-runnable while covering the critical query-execution path.
evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutorIntegrationTest is @Disabled at class level; BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest is @Disabled at class level; DependencySketchTest is @Disabled at class level
The LiveIndex nested class in BlackLabQueryExecutorTest is also @Disabled

Only the five static helper tests in BlackLabQueryExecutorTest (buildBcqlWithLemmaSubstitution variants) run in CI — none of executeCollocations, executeBcqlQuery, executeDependencyPattern, or getTotalFrequency are validated

issues.summary: Per-relation response shape differs across full-sketch, single-relation, and dep-sketch endpoints
issues.suggestion: Define a canonical per-relation response shape (e.g. {id, name, description, cql, collocate_pos_group, total_matches, collocations}) and use it in all three code paths; omit type-specific fields (deprel) via null/absent rather than forking the shape. Extract a static helper buildSurfaceRelationMap(RelationConfig, List, List) so all three callers assemble identical base fields.
evidence: handleFullSketchForType (line ~122-127) puts {name, cql, collocate_pos_group, collocations} — missing 'id' and 'total_matches'
handleRelationQueryForPattern (line ~220-230) puts {id, name, collocations, total_matches} — missing 'cql' and 'collocate_pos_group'
buildRelationResponse (line ~268-277) for dep sketch puts {id, name, description, deprel, total_matches, collocations} — yet another shape

issues.summary: 'relation' field in response bodies echoes enum type name in explore endpoints but relation ID in concordance endpoint
issues.suggestion: Echo the relation ID (e.g. "noun_adj_predicates") consistently in all response bodies. If the relation type (SURFACE/DEP) is also needed, add a separate 'relation_type' field rather than overloading 'relation'. Align nesting: either always embed it under 'parameters' or always at the top level.
evidence: ExplorationHandlers line 223: paramsUsed.put("relation", relationType) where relationType = relationConfig.relationType().orElseThrow().name() — yields e.g. "SURFACE"
ConcordanceHandlers line 77: response.put("relation", req.relation()) where req.relation() is the raw relation ID from the request — yields e.g. "noun_adj_predicates"
Additionally the explore echo is nested under a 'parameters' sub-object while the concordance echo is at the top level

issues.summary: cors.allow.origin=* silently widens browser trust boundary across all endpoints with only a log warning
issues.suggestion: Add a startup check in WordSketchApiServer.start() that reads cors.allow.origin and calls logger.error + System.exit(1) (or throws IllegalStateException) when the value is '*', unless a separate -Dcors.allow.origin.allow_wildcard=true opt-in flag is set. Remove the per-response warn in setCorsHeader once the startup guard exists.
evidence: HttpApiUtils.setCorsHeader() reads cors.allow.origin at call time and sets Access-Control-Allow-Origin=* when the property equals '*', emitting only a logger.warn() — no startup validation prevents this from reaching production silently.
The warning fires per-response (on every setCorsHeader call) rather than once at startup, producing log noise without preventing the misconfiguration.
